free_text: Accumulation of the Hf-178-m2 isomeric nuclei through
spallation with intermediate-energy protons of tantalum
and rhenium targets.
Last checking has been done.
The LANET code simulations.
A coaxial high-purity detector.
The irradiations with the extracted beam were
aimed to perform the absolute calibration of the cross
section values.
The accuracy on the efficiency values of detector was
better than 5%. Systematical errors are due to the use
of the tabulated decay properties of radionuclides.
Finallly, the random errors for the majority of
detected radionuclides to be about 7%. This value does
not include the error on the proton beam intensity and
the calibration of the absolute yield values.
The measured number of radionuclides was transformed in
production cross section. At 660-MeV the absolute cross
section calibration was performed by using the special
combined irradiation of Ta/Re and Al. For the
irradiations at 100, 150, 200, 300 and 450-MeV the
measured yields for the radionuclides W-178, Os-185
and Re-183, that provided intense gamma-rays, could
be used for absolute calibration of the cross sections.
The areas of their gamma-lines were measured with high
statistical accuracy in the spectra taken before
chemical operations. The absolute calibration accuracy
is estimated to be better than 15%.
Three Ta targets of the same thickness, 33.3 g/cm**2
were exposed to the internal beam at different radii
corresponding to incident energies of 660, 200 and
100-MeV. A fourth irradiation was performed using the
extracted 660-MeV proton beam on a target consisting of
a stack of two thin (156 mg/cm**2) foils of Ta-156 and
Al 30.6 mg/cm**2 thickness.  This way the two foils
were exposed to the same beam fluence.
- By authors. Ta-182 production (yield=0.85e-3
atoms/proton) has been obtained due to (n,gamma)
reaction.  Hf-181 has been produced in the reaction
(p,p+pi-mezon).

Three Ta targets of the same thickness, 33.3 g/cm**2
were exposed to the internal beam at different radii
corresponding to incident energies of 660, 200 and
100-MeV. A fourth irradiation was performed using the
extracted 660-MeV proton beam on a target consisting of
a stack of two thin (156 mg/cm**2) foils of Ta-156 and
Al 30.6 mg/cm**2 thickness.  This way the two foils
were exposed to the same beam fluence.
-By authors. For yield value-random errors are within
7% and the standard errors of the absolute calibration
is about +-15%.
Targets of natural Re, 21 g/cm**2 thickness, were
exposed to the internal beam at four different incident
energies-150, 300, 450 and 660-MeV.
A stack of two foils of natural Re and Al of
comparable thickness-0.56 mg/cm**2, were irradiated
with the 660-MeV extracted beam.
-By authors. The measurement accuracy is better than
15% in all cases, with exception for some lowest
yields.
